# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Monday 7/20

**[Monday 7/20 session outlook] Bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +21 · confidence 40%**

**🔮 Next-session forecast** — The next U.S. regular session is Monday, July 20th, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST on 7/20). This report is a forward-looking forecast for that session, prepared while markets are closed.

*Data as of: 2026-07-19T14:36:13 · Data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

With the next U.S. regular session set for Monday, July 20 (opening 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/20), this brief lays out the outlook heading into that trading day. The overall market-state composite reads +21 out of 100, placing conditions in a "Moderate / selective risk-on" regime, though confidence in that read stands at a modest 40%, based on four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured for this cycle — liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning — all of which were reweighted out of the current composite.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Heading into Monday's open, the major benchmarks enter from a softer footing: the S&P 500 last closed at 7,457.69, down 1.01% on the day; the Nasdaq-100 finished at 28,592.66, off 1.49%; the Dow settled at 52,146.42, down 0.77%; and the Russell 2000 closed at 2,962.22, down a comparatively modest 0.42%.

**Market drivers.** The setup into next session is mixed. Volatility is acting as a headwind, as is the prevailing index trend and a firmer US dollar. The one supportive counterweight is the yield curve, which is currently working in the market's favor.

**Breadth & concentration.** The composite market-state score of +21/100 continues to anchor the "Moderate / selective risk-on" characterization, with confidence held at 40% across four measured components. As noted above, liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning are not currently factored into this reading, having been reweighted out.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership into the new week is concentrated in Health Care, Energy, and Financials, while Consumer Discretionary, Materials, and Technology are lagging the tape.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.57%. The dollar index (DXY) is at 100.75, up 0.02% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude has jumped 3.58% to 81.78, and gold has added 0.83% to reach 4,018.80.

## Outlook

**Short term.** Framed specifically for Monday's session, the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 40% confidence level. This view would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance likewise leans constructive, again grounded in the same "Moderate / selective risk-on" reading at 40% confidence, with the same trigger for reversal: a move of the overall score below the zero line.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term bias remains constructive on the same basis — the "Moderate / selective risk-on" state at 40% confidence — and would similarly flip if the overall score crosses zero.
